content:
AI can suggest fixes, explain errors, and generate code in seconds. It still cannot replace the discipline of finding out what is actually wrong.

11 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

AI Cannot Rescue a Developer Who Refuses to InvestigateAI is very good at giving developers something to try.
That is not the same as helping them understand the problem.
A developer pastes an error into a chat window. The AI suggests a change. The error disappears, but another one appears somewhere else. A second prompt produces another patch. Then a third.
Eventually, the feature starts working.
Nobody knows why.
This is where AI-assisted development becomes dangerous. The tool keeps producing answers, so the developer never feels forced to stop and investigate.
But software does not become reliable because enough errors were removed.
It becomes reliable when someone understands what failed, where it failed, and why the fix is correct.
AI can support that work.
It cannot do it for a developer who refuses to look.
AI Makes Avoiding the Problem Feel Productive
